summary.intent: Build a system to link checkpoints to their corresponding git commits by parsing commit messages, storing the mapping in a database, and displaying the resulting file diff in the session detail view.
summary.outcome: Implemented end-to-end: a git commit lookup utility, a `CheckpointIdGitOidJoin` Prisma table, indexer integration to populate mappings, a backend diff endpoint using `git show`, and a `CommitFileTree` React component with collapsible directory tree and file status badges.

repo: Checkpoint IDs are embedded in git commit messages under the key `Entire-Checkpoint: <id>` at the end of the message body.
The indexer has two versions: V1 (`indexCheckpoint`/`indexAllCheckpoints`) and V2 (`indexCheckpointV2`/`indexAllCheckpointsV2`); V1 was deprecated and removed during this session.
The repo path is available as `repoDir` at the server/call-site level and must be threaded down into indexer functions.
All 39 checkpoints were already indexed before the OID mapping feature landed, so the `isNew` guard had to be removed — `saveGitOidMapping` uses its own `findFirst` deduplication instead.
The `execSync` utility was already imported in `src/server.ts`, making it straightforward to shell out to `git show`.
Frontend lives in `app/src/renderer/src/`; API fetch functions are centralized in `api.ts`; components go in `components/`.

code.finding: New file: exports `getCheckpointIdFromCommitMessage` (regex parse) and `findCommitForCheckpoint` (gitlog scan with branch fallback to main/master).
TypeScript narrowing breaks when a boolean alias like `isNew` is used instead of a direct null-check; workaround was a non-null assertion after the if/else block.
`CheckpointIdGitOidJoin` uses `gitOid` as primary key with an index on `checkpointId`; a checkpoint can map to multiple git OIDs.
New endpoint `GET /api/v2/checkpoints/:id/diff` looks up OID via `CheckpointIdGitOidJoin`, runs `git show --no-color <oid>`, returns raw unified diff text.
New component: parses unified diff with `parse-diff`, builds a path-compressed directory tree, renders collapsible dirs and per-file status badges (A/M/D/R) with +/- line counts.

workflow: gitlog npm library requires the repo path to be absolute and passed via the options object; callback-style API is used.
Removing an `isNew` guard in favor of letting the helper's own deduplication logic handle re-runs is a safer pattern when backfilling existing records.
Building the backend (`tsc`) and type-checking the frontend separately catches errors in both layers before restarting the server.
parse-diff was installed in the `app/` subdirectory (the Electron/Vite frontend), not the root package.

friction: gitlog API required significant trial-and-error (9+ bash commands) to figure out correct call shape, ESM vs CJS usage, execOptions, and absolute path requirement.
TypeScript narrowing through a boolean alias (`isNew`) failed silently until build time, requiring a non-null assertion workaround.
Multiple rounds of reading `indexer.ts` were needed due to the file's size and the complexity of threading `repoPath` through V1 and V2 functions simultaneously.
The `isNew` guard caused the OID mapping to never fire for pre-existing checkpoints, only discovered after querying the empty table at runtime.
open_items: `saveGitOidMapping` now runs on every index pass for every checkpoint and does a `findFirst` DB query each time; could be optimized by only running for checkpoints not yet in the join table.
The `CommitFileTree` directory collapse uses `∨/›` characters — may want to replace with proper icon components for visual consistency.
Diff is only fetched and shown for the top (latest) checkpoint in the session detail view; could be extended to any checkpoint.
No error handling or user-facing message when a checkpoint has no linked commit (diff silently absent).
V1 `indexCheckpoint` was removed but `indexAllCheckpoints` now delegates to V2 — worth auditing whether any external callers (plugins, scripts) relied on V1 behavior.
